headline: Inference-efficiency techniques dominate the week as quantization, prompt caching, and distillation cluster around faster generation.
body: Quantization and prompt caching lead and break out simultaneously, anchored by NVIDIA's pipeline that cuts diffusion denoising from fifty steps to as few as one by stacking dynamic quantization, latent chunk caching, and student-teacher distillation for ten to two hundred times speedup on Blackwell B200. Alongside them, advantage weighting, token pruning, and data selection enter as new breakouts, signaling rotation toward training and inference optimization. Multi-agent systems remain active across separate threads: Google Cloud's ADK and A2A game demonstration, IBM-backed Kagenti's identity and delegation-chain security, and Tencent WorkBuddy's domain-expert agent workforce. Prompt-injection defense also leads and breaks out, with discussion of the Fable jailbreak characterized as expected model behavior rather than a security failure.

cited_items.title: You Might Not Need 50 Diffusion Steps — Ziv Ilan, Nvidia
Building with ADK & A2A: How multi-agent collaboration designs a game
Kagenti’s Approach to Multi-Agent Security for AI Agents
Tencent Workbuddy: RIP Claude,Codex! This is ACTUALLY quite a bit CRAZY!
